title: Mark 6
wikitext:
[]: Mark 6 [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jul 28, 2009 10:55 pm So, in Mark 6, here's what happens: -Jesus returns to his hometown, accompanied by his twelve apostles. -He teaches people stuff and heals a few people. -He sends his apostles out to other villages, taking nothing with them but a staff, to heal people. -King Herod finds out about Jesus and believes that John the Baptist, whom he had beheaded, had been raised from the dead. -Jesus leaves with his apostles for a "solitary place" that turns out to have five thousand people. He feeds them. -He sends his apostles to Bethsaida and walks on water. Nothing here seems particularly relevant to Evangelion except for the twelve Apostles, Kaworu returning to Earth with Mark 06 and stopping 3I with a staff, and Kaworu being alive "again" after his "previous" beheading in NGE, but if you give an Evangelion a name that's also a Bible verse, you can't expect for it never to get brought up.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:31 pm Kazuya Tsurumaki wrote: There are a lot of giant robot shows in Japan, and we did want our story to have a religious theme to help distinguish us. Because Christianity is an uncommon religion in Japan we thought it would be mysterious. None of the staff who worked on Eva are Christians. There is no actual Christian meaning to the show, we just thought the visual symbols of Christianity look cool. If we had known Once we knew that the show would get was being distributed in the US and Europe, we might have rethought that choice Anno-san decided that we would introduce even more pointless Judaeo-Christian references in every subsequent Eva production. He's a giant troll, after all.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jul 29, 2009 7:56 pm I thought that Kaworu was John the Baptist, and Shinji was Jesus (well, that's the way I saw it). But this is Rebuild, so things have probably changed. And there's the Johannite heresy, in which followers of John believed he was superior to Jesus. Leonardo da Vinci was apparently one of the Johannites.

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Wed Apr 13, 2011 10:47 am Huh, Axell mentioned this thread to me when we were watching 2.22... Suddenly I remembered that I'd wanted to check out the reference, and naturally upon reading Mark Ch. 6 was astounded (or maybe not so astounded) to find out that it actually fit. I'd bet my money that there are two more Angels remaining if Kaworu's supposed to be Jesus, otherwise there's only one left before him. Obvious reasoning: 12 disciples-> 12 angels 1. Adam 2. Lilith 3. [s:s4tym2o1]Boniel[/s:s4tym2o1] 4. [s:s4tym2o1]Sachiel[/s:s4tym2o1] 5. [s:s4tym2o1]Shamshel[/s:s4tym2o1] 6. [s:s4tym2o1]Ramiel[/s:s4tym2o1] 7. [s:s4tym2o1]Clockiel[/s:s4tym2o1] 8. [s:s4tym2o1]Sahaquiel[/s:s4tym2o1] 9. [s:s4tym2o1]Bardiel[/s:s4tym2o1] 10. [s:s4tym2o1]Zeruel[/s:s4tym2o1] 11. ??? 12. ??? (or not-Jesus Kaworu) Dunno if it's been discussed already (knowing this forum it has XD), but I just thought I'd throw that out there. By the way, I'm baaaaaaack!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Apr 13, 2011 11:18 am From my analysis , I never saw it as a scripture passage, but reference to something written in Revelation: --------------------------------------------- FROM THE ANALYSIS: Anno decides to let the end credits become a small break between that cliff-hanger ending and the extended cliff-hanger ending after the credits, where Kaworu hovers down in a Unit that looks remarkably close to Shinji's Eva Unit 01, labeled the "Mark 6%u2033, and tells Shinji that this time he will show him happiness. There are several theories many have tried to form around this particular event, but this essay only goes into the elements present in the film itself, not speculation into how it applies to the future installments to the franchise. From the elements present in this scene, it seems as though the film is forming a symbolic connection between the Christian version of the Apocalypse as described in The Book of Revelation, with "The Beast" Unit 02 taking on The Beast recorded in Revelation 13, and Kaworu's "Mark 6%u2033 referring to "The Mark of the Beast" which Revelation 13 also describes as the number "666%u2033. Revelation 13: 11 Then I saw another beast coming up out of the earth; and he had two horns like a lamb and he spoke as a dragon. 12 He exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence And he makes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed. 13 He performs great signs, so that he even makes fire come down out of heaven to the earth in the presence of men. 14 And he deceives those who dwell on the earth because of the signs which it was given him to perform in the presence of the beast, telling those who dwell on the earth to make an image to the beast who had the wound of the sword and has come to life. 15 And it was given to him to give breath to the image of the beast, so that the image of the beast would even speak and cause as many as do not worship the image of the beast to be killed. 16 And he causes all, the small and the great, and the rich and the poor, and the free men and the slaves, to be given a mark on their right hand or on their forehead, 17 and he provides that no one will be able to buy or to sell, except the one who has the mark, either the name of the beast or the number of his name. 18 Here is wisdom Let him who has understanding calculate the number of the beast, for the number is that of a man; and his number is six hundred and sixty-six. In Qabbalistic Judaism the number 666 has a different meaning, as it represents the creation and perfection of the world, rather than the Mark of the Beast. According to Qabbalah, the world was created in 6 days, and there are 6 cardinal directions (North, South, East, West, Up, Down), and 6 is the numerical value of one of the letters of God's name, hence the number 666. Where as this metaphor puts the ending in a different symbolic light, this application of the symbolic metaphor doesn't allow for the "Beast Mode" to be metaphorically explored. --------------------------------------------- I could be wrong, but it all seems to make more sense than Kaworu feeding the 5,000. And it also explains the presence of "The Beast" (Unit 02), whereas chapter 6 of the Gospel of Mark does not. Though, any other context from that passage and Evangelion seems to be ignored in favor of using these elements in creating a new mythology for Evangelion. It also just makes sense because both the events in 2.22 and the events in Revelation are apocalyptic in nature. Again, the Gospel of Mark is not.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Apr 13, 2011 11:49 am Bible Verses : The Fan Wank Heart of Darkness
